summary: Do not ship yet: the walking skeleton leaves the tab surface outside the new scrolling seam and the bundle gate does not measure the complete eager popup boundary required by later slices.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Bundle gate measures only one eager chunk
findings.body: The gate sizes the first `global-*` file, but the generated popup eagerly loads `popup-*`, `react-*`, and `global-*`. Code can move into or enlarge another eager chunk while the gate still passes. The deferred-chunk allowlist also recognizes only sortable/motion, so it cannot verify ticket 03's explicit Autocomplete lazy-loading contract without extending filename folklore. This undermines the principal structural constraint for the next, bundle-sensitive slice.
findings.recommendation: Derive the popup's eager dependency set from generated `popup.html` or build metadata, gate the aggregate eager size, and assert that feature chunks such as Autocomplete are not reachable eagerly.

findings.title: Tab surface cannot satisfy ScrollArea's height contract
findings.body: `ScrollArea` requires a definite outer height, but the tab shell supplies only `min-h-screen`. An auto-sized grid row can expand with its content, leaving no overflow for the Base UI viewport to own; scrolling then falls back to the document/native scrollbar. The shared popup/tab shell therefore has inconsistent scrolling behavior, and later UI slices will build on a primitive that is not actually wired across both claimed surfaces.
findings.recommendation: Give the tab shell a definite viewport height such as `h-screen`, propagate `min-h-0` through its grid items, and add a tab-surface overflow assertion proving Base UI owns vertical scrolling.

next_steps: Fix both structural seams while the primitive and gate are still isolated.
Re-run bundle-gate, smoke, Storybook, and ui-diag after adding the eager-graph and tab-overflow assertions.
